question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that lagByCluster.remove(localCluster) at line 86 mutates the map before bestOtherLag() is called, by injecting a Prometheus response where the localCluster key also appears as an other-cluster key (via case-sensitivity differences), causing the wrong entry to be removed and bestOtherLag() to use the local lag as the best other lag, preventing DOWN status?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response where the localCluster name differs by case (e.g., 'Mainnet-1' vs 'mainnet-1'), causing lagByCluster.get(localCluster) to return null due to case-sensitive HashMap lookup, making isLagAboveThreshold() return false and hiding importer lag on staking reward epoch processing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of authentication on the Spring Boot Actuator health endpoint to repeatedly poll health() and observe UP/DOWN transitions, inferring when the importer is lagging on staking reward processing, and timing transactions to exploit stale pending_reward values before they are updated?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a denial-of-service against the Prometheus endpoint to keep health() returning UP indefinitely (via the exception catch block), preventing the monitor from detecting importer lag, and allowing stale staking reward data to be served to clients who rely on pending_reward values for financial decisions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: buildLagQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user who can influence the clusters list inject a cluster name containing the pipe character '|' to alter the clusterRegex, causing the PromQL regex to match unintended clusters and return inflated lag data for the other cluster, making isOtherClearlyBetter() return false and suppressing DOWN status while the importer lags on staking reward processing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response where the localCluster lag is just above thresholdSeconds but all other cluster lags are also just above thresholdSeconds + margin, causing isOtherClearlyBetter() to return false and health() to return UP, masking a lagging importer that has not processed staking reward transfers and allowing stale reward data to persist?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a large number of Prometheus series entries with unique cluster names to cause memory pressure in the HashMap built by parseLagByCluster(), triggering an OutOfMemoryError that propagates as an Exception caught by health(), which then returns UP, masking importer lag on staking reward processing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: toClusterLagEntry()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus series where value is an empty list (size 0), causing the value.size() < 2 check in toClusterLagEntry() to return Optional.empty(), removing the localCluster from the lag map and causing health() to return UP while the importer is lagging on staking reward record ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: isOtherClearlyBetter()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response where the best other cluster lag is Double.MAX_VALUE, causing bestOther.getAsDouble() + margin to overflow to Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY, making the < comparison with localLag return false and preventing DOWN status, masking importer lag on staking reward processing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition where health() is called concurrently, and the Prometheus response is partially processed, causing evaluate() to operate on an inconsistent lagByCluster map and return UP when it should return DOWN, masking importer lag on staking reward epoch-day processing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response where the series list contains only entries for non-local clusters with very high lag values, causing bestOtherLag() to return a high value, making isOtherClearlyBetter() return false and health() return UP, masking the local importer's lag on staking reward processing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: toClusterLagEntry()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus series where metric is null, causing toClusterLagEntry() to return Optional.empty() (line 120 null check), removing the localCluster from the lag map and causing health() to return UP while the importer is lagging on staking reward record processing?
